page: OVHcloud r3-1024 is a memory-optimized server instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 128 dedicated physical cores powered by AMD EPYC processors running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz, with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio. The system is equipped with 1024.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage consists of a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is supported by a 20 Gbps baseline bandwidth. The instance runs on a KVM hypervisor and includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores all placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory performance is also highly rated, with cached read-only small block memory bandwidth reaching 9921.46 GB/sec (top 10%) and uncached large block read/write bandwidths ranking in the top 25%. Database operations and Redis SET operations are highly efficient, with Redis achieving 166.68 million operations per second. However, single-threaded tasks, such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, perform in the middle 50% tier.

From a resource density perspective, the r3-1024 offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads due to its massive 1 TB memory allocation paired with dedicated, non-hyperthreaded physical cores. A key tradeoff is the lack of a dedicated GPU, which limits its utility for certain workloads. While prompt processing for large language models ranks in the top 25%, actual text generation speeds for a 70B model fall into the bottom 10% (2.26 tokens/sec), indicating that CPU-only execution is inefficient for large-scale generative AI.

This server is configured for memory-intensive and highly parallelized workloads. Appropriate use cases include large-scale in-memory databases, Redis caching layers, heavy multi-threaded software compilation, and data processing pipelines that require substantial RAM without GPU acceleration.
description: OVHcloud r3-1024 is a memory-optimized server featuring 128 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores on the x86_64 architecture, with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio and 2.3 GHz clock speed. It is equipped with 1024.0 GB of RAM, a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and a 20 Gbps baseline network. Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, alongside strong database and Redis operations. However, single-threaded performance and large-model LLM text generation are limited by the lack of hyperthreading and GPU acceleration. This instance is designed for memory-intensive, highly parallel workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and multi-threaded software compilation.

tagline: A memory-optimized server featuring one terabyte of RAM and one hundred twenty-eight dedicated physical cores for parallel data processing.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio.
1024 GB of RAM providing 8.0 GB of memory capacity per core.
400 GB local NVMe SSD and 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and Redis performance.
Suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and parallel data pipelines.
